title: search — compliance evidence
description: Search indexes user + tenant content — the compliance surface centres on (a) not indexing restricted-tier fields, (b) tenant-scope isolation on queries, (c) query-text retention with GDPR-friendly hashing, (d) right-to-erasure cascades. Sync-job transitions dual-write to audits; queries do not (query volume is telemetry, not audit).

controls.evidence: HasSearchable::toSearchableArray() opts-in fields via #[SearchField] declarations — restricted-tier fields are never indexed. Enforcement is a compile-time check on the trait implementation.

SearchSyncJob rows tier-based retention (90/180/365d). SearchAnalyticsEvent rows tier-based short retention (30/90/365d). Raw query text scrubbed to NULL after 7d independent of row TTL (query_text_days config). See retention.json.

user::UserErased listener (AnonymiseSearchForErasedUser) nulls SearchAnalyticsEvent.user_id, transfers or soft-deletes SearchSavedQuery, anonymises SearchSyncJob.causer_id. Physical index documents are removed via the model's own erasure listener writing to the search index through HasSearchable's `deleted` observer.
Every #[Searchable] entity is also #[Exportable] via the transfer module — the machine-readable copy is served by /api/v1/transfer/exports, not by search.

Tenant scope is applied server-side to every query — even Search::engine('meilisearch')->raw() calls have the tenant filter injected. query_hash uses SHA-256 so top-queries aggregation works without exposing raw text. IP addresses of query originators NOT stored.
search_sync_jobs is a light records-of-processing surface for admins (who reindexed what, when). The heavy version is in `audit` for reindex + flush + synonym mutations.

TLS to every engine. Meilisearch + ES API keys stored via config env vars (never in DB). pgvector runs inside the tenant Postgres cluster (same security posture as data-at-rest). Rate limiting via search.rate-limit prevents scraping. `search.query.max_length` + query_hash prevent abuse of raw storage.
Export machinery serves CCPA data-subject-access-requests. Search-analytics events referencing the user are included.
Same erasure listener as GDPR Art. 17.
regulated_minor tier fields never indexed. Search results referencing minor records are gated by the source entity's viewability permission (per-entity `requiredPermission` on #[Searchable]).
Per-entity `requiredPermission` on #[Searchable] filters models the caller cannot see. Tenant scope + policy composed at every entry point (HTTP + Search::query() facade).
Metrics in metrics.json. Health probes for engine reachability + sync worker freshness + config drift + stalled jobs. Alias swap failure pages ops.
Every reindex / flush / synonym mutation writes an activity row for tenant admin visibility.
config_hash drift detection surfaces attribute-driven schema changes; deploys that ship #[SearchField] changes without a follow-up reindex are flagged on admin dashboards.

controls.principle: privacy protection in the processing of PII
controls.evidence: restricted-tier fields never indexed; tenant scope enforced server-side; raw query text scrubbed at 7d retaining only query_hash; per-entity permission composed with search visibility.

Retention scan (PruneSearchAnalyticsJob) hard-deletes analytics rows past TTL, nulls raw query text at 7d, anonymises on user erasure. TenantErased cascades to physical engine indexes without swap grace.
